title: Plikk
content:
{{Top|leg|notoc=1}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Plikk.jpg]] |name=Plikk |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[Star Wars (1977) 46|''Star Wars'' (1977) 46]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin=Light |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|For years, Captain, we have patrolled this sector—seeing few battles—gaining fewer glories. But I have a feeling about this...!"
"As do I, Nizzon. As though discovery of what lies beyond that rent will bring us all the glories of a lifetime.|Nizzon and Plikk|}} [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] '''Plikk''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Officer/Legends|officer]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] who commanded a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] operating in a remote, backwater [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. Along with her [[Executive officer/Legends|First Officer]], [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Nizzon]], Plikk quietly patrolled this sector for [[Standard year/Legends|years]] without seeing much action, before stumbling upon a curious dimensional rift in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. Plikk led her fleet through the rent to find the [[City of Dreams]], a utopian dreamscape psychically created by [[Cody Sunn-Childe]], a former [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] hero who had mysteriously disappeared years earlier. Desperately wanting the glory that been denied to her for so long, Plikk ordered her ships to attack and destroy the city, using the last of the remaining power in their [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrives]] to fuel the assault. However, this left Plikk and her fleet stranded in Sunn-Childe's pocket dimension, with no way out. ==Behind the scenes== Plikk first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 46|''Star Wars'' (1977) 46]], written by [[John Marc DeMatteis]] and released in [[1981]]. In the issue, Plikk was illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]]. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (1977) 46|''Star Wars'' (1977) 46]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 46}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Navy captains of the Galactic Empire]]
